interpretation_note: Hard regime (12% revise-helpful). Best practical policy (v7) gains +3pp over greedy at avg cost 1.46. Oracle upper bound is 0.91 at cost 1.12 — shows room to improve routing. Budget frontier reaches oracle-level accuracy (0.91) at cost 1.2, and near-oracle (0.89) already at cost 1.1 (+10pp over greedy with only 10% cost overhead).

Hard regime replication (9% revise-helpful). Adaptive v5 matches always-revise accuracy (0.91) at 46% lower cost. Oracle is 0.92 at cost 1.09. Budget frontier achieves oracle-level accuracy at cost 1.1 — the strongest single-step lift across all datasets (+9pp over greedy at just 10% cost overhead).

Mixed-signal regime (6% revise-helpful). Revise does not improve over reasoning for average items; adaptive policy v6 offers marginal +1pp at very low cost overhead (1.03). Oracle (0.70) and budget frontier at cost 1.1 both reach 0.70 — headroom exists but requires near-perfect routing signal.

Easy regime (2% revise-helpful). Adaptive v6 matches always-revise accuracy (0.92) at cost 1.18 vs 2.00. Oracle ceiling is already at 0.92. Budget frontier is flat above cost 1.1 — virtually all accuracy gain occurs in the first 10% cost step; further spending yields no benefit.
